Twice there was someone trying to enter our room in the middle of the night. Thank god we locked it and put the extra lock thing on the door. I wouldn’t recommend staying here if it’s more than a couple days...

Sentence: The pros of this property are it is very spacius and reasonably clean. But the negatives are extensive being the hot water in the main bathroom was non existant. The shower produced only cold water. In the second bathrrom the shower head leaked from the top of the head which is a pain as it sprays into your face while showering but at least the waterwas hot.The second bedroom has wallpaper peeling off the wall. The fridge had a nasty odour eminationg when I opened the door and never cooled anything put in it even overnight the best it could do was lukewarm. But whatever you do, do not use the washing machine as it ruined 6 T-shirts that I had the misfortune to put in it to be washed. The came out filthy with stains and were destroyed. In fact I had to buy new ones to replace them. Considering this property bills itself as a 5 star property I most certainly do no think it is. I needs refurbishing to bring it up to that level. After all mismatched cheap sofa and chairs are not a good look and does not help to project a luxury expereience as claimed when advertising the property. So a little honesty would not go amiss here. So be warned I recomend with some very considerable reservations.

Sentence: First of all, collecting the key wasn’t actually an issue. The pickup location was in the building directly opposite the apartment, so it was only a short walk away. However, the problems started when I checked in. The smart key card simply didn’t work. After dragging my luggage around and finally getting to the unit, I ended up waiting outside the room for about 20 minutes. I contacted customer service via WhatsApp. They did reply, but usually every 5 minutes or so, which felt quite slow considering I was locked out of the room and waiting in the hallway with my luggage. What frustrated me most was that they kept asking me to tap the card again and again, as if I had never used a key card before. They also repeatedly asked for videos, which I sent multiple times. Despite all that, the card still didn’t work. In the end, I was only able to enter using the door code instead of the smart key. If guests are expected to collect their own keys and handle a self check-in process, then at the very least everything should work properly when they arrive. Making sure the key card actually functions seems like the bare minimum. The room itself seemed fine at first, until I found two fake eyelashes stuck inside the blanket. Honestly, that was pretty disgusting. It immediately made me question whether the bedding had actually been changed or washed properly between guests. Not a great impression when it comes to cleanliness and hygiene. Another thing I found strange was that there were no facial tissues provided in the unit. Maybe it’s a small thing, but it’s something I normally expect from an Airbnb. Having to use toilet paper for everything felt a bit cheap and inconvenient. The elevator situation was also frustrating. For a building with more than 40 floors, having only three lifts serving each wing is simply not enough. During my stay, I regularly waited close to 10 minutes just to go up or down, which became quite annoying very quickly. Would I recommend staying here? Personally, no. There were just too many avoidable issues for me to overlook. I won’t be returning, and I’d suggest future guests think twice before booking.

Sentence: We booked the Executive 2-Bedroom. Apartment and had a wonderful stay. The unit came with two bedrooms and two bathrooms, with one bedroom having an attached bathroom. This was my first apartment-style stay experience, and it definitely exceeded my expectations. The apartment was located on the 33rd floor at Block A, and the check-in process was smooth. We collected our keys from the designated check-in counter, and although check-in and check-out were self-service, the staff were friendly and helpful whenever assistance was needed. One of the biggest advantages of this property is its location. TRX Mall is only about a 5-minute walk away. Within just a few minutes’ walk, there are plenty of food options including Chinese restaurants, mamak eateries, durian stalls, and even a McDonald’s nearby. Getting a Grab was also very easy and convenient. The apartment itself was very new, clean, and comfortable. The air-conditioning worked perfectly, and I especially liked that we could adjust the room temperature to our preference. The unit was well-equipped with kitchen utensils, including knives, scissors, and other essentials. There was also a washing machine available for guests who wish to do laundry. Netflix was provided in the living room, which was a nice bonus. There were no TVs in the bedrooms, but that wasn’t an issue for us. The only minor things to note are that facial tissues and laundry detergent were not provided, so it would be good to bring your own if needed. Overall, this was an excellent stay. The apartment was clean, comfortable, well-located, and great value for money. I would definitely stay here again and highly recommend it to anyone visiting the TRX area.

Sentence: This apartment is suitable for anyone who looking for a spacious place, walking distance to Bukit Bintang area, especially TRX Exchange. Alot of shopping, food area within a short walking distance. Its literally 5-10 minutes walk to TRX and Bukit Bintang areas, however, there is no dedicated area for pedestrian on the way to TRX. But to Bukit Bintang, it is convenience. We got 59th floor unit on our stay, just below the roof top 60th floor. Its so high that the wait and time at the elevator is not as convenient as you can take more than 10 mins just to wait for the lift and going down to the lobby. Would suggest communicate in advance to the guest to check their comfort level, or can also price it differently in the expense of convenience. In anyway, we don;t see any "view" or other value to stay in the high floor. The unit itself, marketed as japandi design, quite spacious living room, but very small - no window - second bedroom. On the day we arrived, it was heavy rain and there was water leakage from the window in the living room. Apartment facility is pretty complete, washing machine, full kitchen, large fridge, super high speed internet. However, during our stay, they washing machine was broken. On cleanliness, hope they can regularly replace the bed sheet (it was brown color bedsheet due to the japandi design), while it may be cleaned but can easily see stained on the brown color bedsheet. Last point, I think they need to better invest on the bed mattress on the main bedroom. I believe people come for the sleeping comfort. Can clearly say it was a cheap mattress, can feel the spring when you sleep on it. A decent mattress would definitely improve guest comfort.

Sentence: STAY AWAY! It will ruin your holidays! We had an awful experience at Deface Platinum in Kuala Lumpur. When we first arrived, the check-in process took almost two hours! It was so frustrating, and when we finally got to our room, it was infested with bed bugs. We immediately informed the staff, but instead of helping us right away, they told us we’d have to wait until the next day for a room change. This is completely unacceptable, especially considering there were also ants all over the apartment. To make things worse, the hotel demanded a hefty 500 RM deposit, which they promised to return within 30 business days after the stay. That’s an incredibly long time to wait for a refund! On top of that, we were four people, yet they only gave us one key to the entire apartment. How is that even possible? The pool area was always overcrowded, and there are restricted times to access it, requiring you to have a key to get in. This place really needs to rethink their service and facilities. I would not recommend this hotel to anyone, as my experience was nothing short of a disaster.
